frameworks.name: recurrent neural network (RNN)
frameworks.one_line: trained RNNs as mechanistic models of value learning and choice

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning over feature and conjunction values
frameworks.one_line: value estimated by combining feature-level and conjunction-level reward probabilities

frameworks.name: distributed neural coding
frameworks.one_line: value and strategy emerge from distributed population activity in RNN

frameworks.name: excitatory/inhibitory circuit dynamics
frameworks.one_line: distinct roles of E and I units support the mixed learning strategy

core_question: How do neural circuits represent value and adopt learning strategies in multi-dimensional probabilistic environments with many options and features?
key_assumptions: value of a stimulus can be decomposed into informative features and their conjunctions
RNN dynamics can capture human learning and choice behavior
learning strategy is shaped by structure of the reward environment
manipulations: multi-dimensional probabilistic learning task with feature-level reward structure
lesioning of RNN units (including E vs I populations)
analysis of RNN representations and connectivity
key_findings: participants combined informative-feature reward estimates with informative-conjunction estimates
RNNs trained on the task reproduced this mixed learning strategy
mixed strategy relied on distributed value codes in the RNN
inhibitory and excitatory units made distinct contributions to value learning
interpretations: value representations and learning strategies emerge from distributed circuits with specialized E/I roles
feature-plus-conjunction decomposition is a viable solution to high-dimensional credit assignment
connecting_signals: recurrent neural network model of choice
